item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome!

item.title: Re: What's the blue text say?
item.content:encoded:
I really don't think that I'm colorblind, but I feel like this did something weird to my eyes. When not looking directly at a flashing word/letter, I could see that something in that area was pulsing blue. When I looked directly at the area where I thought the pulsing was happening, I couldn't see it, but then I could see other words/letters pulsing elsewhere on the page. I'd try focusing really hard and then it would seem like nothing was pulsing at all, then I'd stop focusing so hard and I'd see the pulsing all over the text again. It was pretty much impossible for me to identify which specific letters were blinking unless they were the first or last letters in a word.

I thought this was intentional. If it was intentional, I'd say, "usually I dig subtlety, but this optical gimmick is too hard to parse, make it more obvious." But, coming to the discussion thread, I see that the only other person having this issue is colorblind. So instead I'll say, "Holy crap, have I been colorblind for my whole life and not realized it until now?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is definitely one of my favorite skips on the site. I liked it when it was just mind control helmets but WOW the attached files made it even better. Solid work here, and lots of love for Eric.

+1

item.title: Re: What's the blue text say?
item.content:encoded: They aren't just links, I'd recommend reading though again and waiting a bit on that last document. It's some fantastic work they've done.

item.title: Re: What's the blue text say?
item.content:encoded:
What the blue text says is under the “Spoilers” collapsible in my author post.

If there’s enough demand, I’ll make a colorblind version where it just bolds it instead.

item.title: Re: What's the blue text say?
item.content:encoded:
/tmp/unzip/omega-incident-docs/
├── laster_recovery_report.pdf
├── incident-log-4688-3.pdf
├── scp-4688-2017-09-30.docx
├── maxwll-eric-commlog.odt
└── scp-4688-2017-11-05.docx

They are just links? that open up incident reports under them. Hope this helped

item.title: What's the blue text say?
item.content:encoded: I've spent way too long trying to figure out the blue text but I'm just lost. I love a good format screw, but it kind of sucks for people who are colorblind or use screen readers.

item.title: Re: Thank you
item.content:encoded: Awwww thank you so much! That means so much!

item.title: Re: Thank you
item.content:encoded: That means a lot :-)

item.title: Thank you
item.content:encoded:
This is legit one of the best SCPs I've read in fucking ages. I started out like "Oh mind control helmets, this is right up my alley and was ready to upvote it, then I got to the logs and stuff at the bottom and actually got confused by the interface before realising that I could actually click the blue titles and read more logs about it.
Then when I noticed something, I thought it was imagination because it's late here, but I noticed it again and actually had to get out my pen and paper to write down what I saw and it blew my mind. By the end I was empathizing with some characters and wanting to beat up others.
Thank you both for writing this and putting a smile on my face on a day that I really needed cheering up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Omg thank you so much! It was a tough balance, keeping Eric’s core elements while still making the story interesting. But we did it! I love the Eric GOI, and I plan on writing some more new Eric articles :)

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: It’s team bird primarily because of Eta-4, which is pretty exclusive to that canon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. Is. Wonderful.

Strong narrative, excellent pacing, and the amount of work involved with the formatting and media elements? Mew, Seagull, really, excellent job!

I was wary at first, reviving a concept/character from Series I, but this article manages to feel like a Series I object that's been fleshed out into Series V style storytelling, and it does it so seamlessly! The mystery unfolds with just the right amount of elements, both in terms of interface and addendum. No one part of the article felt like it overstayed it's welcome, and all of it enhanced the story.

No question +1 from me. Easily my favorite collab to come out this month.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! All that fuckery and extra files was Seagull’s idea, and it was fucking awesome. This thing was so fun to put together. I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
I can’t answer the Team Bird question, that’s gonna have to be Seagull lol.

As for the compliments, thank you so much! I wanted to use Eric in this contest because I wanted to expand upon his story more, give him a little more character. The idea Seagull had happened to make that pretty easy! Thanks again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was off to a good start.

…and then that extra layer added in the revision.

…and then that subtle format fuckery in the final page.

…AND THEN THE REST OF THE FILES AFTER THAT.

Big fat +1 on a foam finger because that was god damn cool as hell.

Wow, I didn't know that Eric could be… expanded like that. +1

But I don't get the team-bird tag. I didn't read 4800 but the only reason I can think is because of Calvin

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything here just works really well. Super easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo thanks my dude

When I first saw it I thought the same thing I zoomed in close and stuff haha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That second to last log was trippy as fuck my dudes I thought my eyes were going wonky.

Very well done, and a pretty solid +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWEEE THANKS!! That means so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I feel bad for poor Eric, too. I plan on using him in some more SCPs, and more wholesome ones at that, that will reference this chain of events. So, that should be fun. I wrote most of the Eric portions, so that means a lot! And Seagull did fantastic with all the mind boggling shit, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, dude! This was awesome in the chat and it's awesome now. Instant upvote, this'll be a modern classic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Just, wow. You guys blew this absolutely out the water! I really love skips that are more than just what meets the eye, and this really flexes with that idea.

Fantastic work to you all, This is beautiful. Now I have to cry a little, that poor kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
bro what even HAPPENED here

im gonna reread it tomorrow morning when im not dead

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus H. Christ on a corn cob-

You know, I'm a sucker for skips that start out "this is a simple object" right before swinging a metaphorical right hook into something I didn't expect.

Just a silly little helmet, yeah? Yeah?

I ain't gonna spoil it for any of you reading through the comments before reading the skip, but…

You'll get it when you see it~!

also yeah you get an upboat

item.title: Re: Author Post - Reviewers and Thanks
item.content:encoded:
TEAM BIRB BEST TEAM

Since it was a point of contention on SCP-4800, which has a similar screw…

+ Spoilers
- Spoilers

The flashing blue text (that exists) on the last page is telling you to go back to the first document (the recovery log). If you go back to the first doc after the last doc, the text will change.

This was fun af to put together, so shout-out to Mew-ltiverse for getting me to do this.

+ Images
- Security Memetic: Eisenhower wasn't that good of a president
  • helmet.jpg is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Childrens_bike_helmet.jpg
  • avatar.jpg is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Avatar_Music_Band-17.jpg
  • helmet2.jpg is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bike_helmet_with_Google_logo_1.jpg
  • google.jpg is a combination of https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tinus_van_Doorn_-_Surrealist_landscape.jpg and the google homepage
  • maninthecomputer.mp4 is my own original work, featuring:
    • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Icons-mini-page_tree.gif
    • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Thistletown_Collegiate_Institute%27s_hallway.jpg
    • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NTS_-_Plumbob_-_Stokes_test_with_Goodyear_ZSG-3_blimp.jpg
    • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Smiley-3D.png
    • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Microwaved_disks-cover_fractal_trees-scann_PNr%C2%B00050.jpg
    • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1026903/scp-wallpapers#post-2112350
    • The laptop that I've written most of my SCPs on. Thought that I'd give it a role.
  • gulls.jpg is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seagulls_and_wires_-_panoramio.jpg

There was also a draft in here. I don't know whose it is, but I stuffed it in here just in case they want it back: http://topia.wikidot.com/scp-4688-draft

item.title: Author Post - Reviewers and Thanks
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to these dudes for the crit: N_Aepic_Fael, TrustyOlValet2, Ihp, TheBlueHour, and pxdnbluesoul

Team Birb, well the Team Birb for Collab-Con came together really quickly, I’d been partner searching for three hours and then Seagull showed up, and this iteration of Team Birb was born. A Mew is even a type of Seagull. It was fate.

I wanted to expand on Eric’s lore, cause I love that kid, and he hasn’t gotten any love in 10 years. His thing is being a kid that has anomalous items, so we tried not to stray too far from that.

I think we’ve got a crazy enough entry for collab con, Maxwellism and Eric, only Team Birb would do that. This concept doesn’t work, but to me it does.

Thanks for reading!
